questions.q: In which Indian city does Purana Qila stand?
questions.explanation: Purana Qila is one of the oldest forts in Delhi, the national capital of India.

questions.q: What does the name 'Purana Qila' mean in English?
questions.explanation: 'Purana Qila' literally translates to 'Old Fort', reflecting its status as Delhi's oldest fort.

questions.q: Purana Qila is most closely associated with which legendary ancient city believed to have stood on its site?
questions.explanation: The site of Purana Qila has long been linked with Indraprastha, the legendary capital of the Pandavas from the Mahabharata.

questions.q: Which Afghan ruler is credited with much of Purana Qila's construction?
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri, founder of the Sur dynasty, raised much of the fort during his short reign.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor began work at the Purana Qila site before being driven from power?
questions.explanation: Humayun started building on the site, naming his city Dinpanah, before Sher Shah Suri ousted him.

questions.q: What was the principal building material used for the walls and gateways of Purana Qila?
questions.explanation: The fort's massive walls and gates were built largely of sandstone, with some marble inlay accents.

questions.q: What name did Humayun give to the city he founded at the Purana Qila site?
questions.explanation: Humayun called his planned city Dinpanah, meaning 'refuge of faith' or 'sanctuary of religion'.

questions.q: Which body of water historically bordered Purana Fort, forming part of its defences?
questions.explanation: The fort originally stood beside the Yamuna, whose waters fed its moat and protected its eastern flank.

questions.q: The mosque inside Purana Qila built by Sher Shah Suri is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Qila-i-Kuhna Masjid, built by Sher Shah Suri, is the principal mosque within Purana Qila.

questions.q: Roughly how many large gateways give access to Purana Qila?
questions.explanation: The fort has three main double-storeyed gateways: the Bara Darwaza, the Humayun Gate, and the Talaqi Gate.

questions.q: What is the name of the octagonal tower at Purana Qila linked to Humayun's death?
questions.explanation: The Sher Mandal, a red sandstone octagonal tower, is said to have served as Humayun's library and the place of his fatal fall.

questions.q: How is the Mughal emperor Humayun believed to have died at Purana Qila?
options: Fell down the steps of the Sher Mandal
questions.explanation: Humayun is said to have stumbled and fallen down the stairs of the Sher Mandal, dying of his injuries in 1556.

questions.q: The Qila-i-Kuhna Mosque is an early example of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The mosque blends Afghan and emerging Mughal elements, an Indo-Islamic style noted for its fine arches and inlay.

questions.q: Which main gate of Purana Qila is the one used by visitors today?
questions.explanation: The Bara Darwaza, or 'Big Gate', on the western side is the entrance used by visitors.

questions.q: The Talaqi Darwaza of Purana Qila is popularly translated as which 'forbidden' gate?
questions.explanation: Talaqi Darwaza is commonly rendered as the 'Forbidden Gate', though the origin of the name is uncertain.

questions.q: Archaeological excavations at Purana Qila have unearthed Painted Grey Ware, a pottery associated with which period?
questions.explanation: Painted Grey Ware found here points to an iron-age settlement, lending support to the site's deep antiquity.

questions.q: During the 1947 Partition, Purana Qila was used for what purpose?
questions.explanation: After Partition, the fort sheltered thousands of Muslim refugees awaiting migration to Pakistan.

questions.q: Which national agency is responsible for the conservation of Purana Qila?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) maintains and conserves Purana Qila as a protected monument.

questions.q: Sher Shah Suri, the chief builder of Purana Qila, is also famous for which major infrastructure achievement?
options: Reviving and extending the Grand Trunk Road
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri is renowned for restoring and extending the Grand Trunk Road and for administrative reforms like the rupiya coin.

questions.q: What feature surrounded the walls of Purana Qila for added defence?
questions.explanation: A wide moat, once fed by the Yamuna, ringed the fort's walls as a defensive barrier.

questions.q: Approximately how long are the perimeter walls of Purana Qila?
questions.explanation: The fort's massive ramparts run for roughly 1.5 kilometres around its irregular oblong plan.

questions.q: After ousting Humayun, Sher Shah Suri renamed the citadel and his city by what name?
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri called his new city Shergarh, building over and around Humayun's Dinpanah.

questions.q: The Qila-i-Kuhna Mosque is notable for its prayer hall featuring how many arched bays (mihrab arches)?
questions.explanation: The single-aisle prayer hall of the Qila-i-Kuhna Masjid is fronted by five elegant arched openings.

questions.q: In which year is Sher Shah Suri generally recorded to have died, leaving Purana Qila partly unfinished?
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri died in 1545 at the siege of Kalinjar, and the fort works were continued by his successors.

questions.q: Humayun first occupied the Dinpanah site around which year before losing it to Sher Shah Suri?
questions.explanation: Humayun laid out Dinpanah around 1533, but Sher Shah Suri drove him out of Delhi within a few years.

questions.q: The colour palette of the Qila-i-Kuhna Mosque is admired for combining red sandstone with inlays of which contrasting stones?
questions.explanation: The mosque's arches are richly decorated with red sandstone set off by white and black marble inlay.

questions.q: The lake beside Purana Qila, popular for boating today, was originally part of which historical feature?
questions.explanation: The boating lake along the western wall is a surviving stretch of the fort's old defensive moat.

questions.q: The Sher Mandal, where Humayun fell, is most often described as having which geometric ground plan?
questions.explanation: The Sher Mandal is a two-storeyed octagonal tower of red sandstone topped by an octagonal pavilion.

questions.q: Excavations at Purana Qila have revealed a continuous human occupation traced back roughly how many centuries?
questions.explanation: Layers at the site indicate occupation stretching back more than two thousand years, well into the pre-Mauryan era.

questions.q: What is the correct status of Purana Qila with respect to UNESCO World Heritage listing?
options: A UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1983
An ASI-protected monument that is not a UNESCO World Heritage Site
questions.explanation: Purana Qila is a nationally protected ASI monument but, unlike Delhi's Red Fort, Qutb Minar, and Humayun's Tomb, it is not on the UNESCO World Heritage list.
